name: Crackling Rice Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
Rice Cakes: Use cooked leftover rice that is slightly moist and sticky. If too dry, reheat with a sprinkling of water until it becomes sticky. Spread the rice in a frying pan or pie tin, about 1/2 inch thick. Pack the rice into firm cakes, pressing firmly to ensure the rice holds together. Place the cakes in an oven on low heat and dry them to a slightly brittle texture; break into pieces. Ten minutes before combining with the soup, deep-fry the rice pieces until they are lightly browned. Strain, place the cakes in the soup tureen, and keep hot in the oven. Avoid holding the fried pieces for more than 10 minutes, as they tend to acquire a rancid taste and become overly dry.
Vegetarian Soup: Wash, then soak soybeans overnight in enough water to cover them completely. Wash and soak Nami mushrooms in one cup of warm water for one hour. When the mushrooms are soft, separate the caps from the stems. In a large soup pot, combine the water, beans, mushrooms (including stems), onion, and garlic. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for two hours. Strain and reserve the stock until ready to finish. Save some mushroom caps and half of the beans for the soup.
To Finish Soup: Thinly slice the mushroom caps and combine them with the strained stock, cooked soybeans, soy sauce, sherry wine, sesame oil, salt, and sugar. Peel the carrot and make thin flower slices (or use a flower cutter); add to the soup. Bring the soup to just under a boil. Add MSG and snow peas. Cook for two minutes.
To Serve: Seat your diners and place the bowl of freshly fried, hot rice pieces on the table. Pour the hot soup over the rice. If the rice and soup are very hot, they will sizzle and crackle to everyone's enjoyment.
